summary: Martin Fowler briefly highlights the Photran project at UIUC, which aims to bring refactoring capabilities to Fortran. He directs readers to Brian Foote's entertaining write-up about the project. The post is essentially a quick link recommendation with Fowler's characteristic wit about Foote's writing habits.
key_points: UIUC is developing refactoring tools for Fortran through the Photran project
Brian Foote has written about the project in his distinctive, humorous style
Fowler notes that getting Foote to write is exceptionally difficult despite his talent
money_quotes: Getting him to write anything is usually like trying to pull teeth from a live sabre-tooth tiger while wearing a necklace of freshly killed lamb chops.
key_insight: Even legacy languages like Fortran can benefit from modern refactoring tooling.
